REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF BERRY JOE CORPORATION'S PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING ACTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST ISSUE AS IT IS SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION CLAUSE PER PARAGRAPH 5(4). THIS APPEARS STORE, FOODS ETC & BERRY JOE CORPORATION WHOSE LEASE DOES NOT EXPIRE UNTIL NOVEMBER, IS IN CLEAR LAKE - NON-APPLICABLE. CLAUSE IN LEASE WHICH IS BEING ENFORCED BY COURT IS "IF PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO AGREE UPON SUCH AN INDEPENDENT APPRAISER, EITHER PARTY MAY REQUEST AAA (AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION) IN SAN FRANCISCO TO APPOINT SUCH AN INDEPENDENT APPRAISER." COURT INSTRUCTS DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER TODAY. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Consistent With The Arbitration Award Of David G. Finkelstein
Off Calendar Jun-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Raymond Choy, (415)387-5148 Defendant/Opposing party on his own behalf.
No other appearances.

The Court inquires if the Defendant complied with the tentative ruling rule. The defendant did not comply. The Defendant is not allowed to argue. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF BERRY JOE CORPORATION'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Consistent With The Arbitration Award Of David G. Finkelstein is GRANTED. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Off Calendar Oct-23-2017 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION For Court To Select And Appointment Of Arbitrator And Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Griffith (530-812-1000) for Plaintiff/Moving party BERRY JOE CORPORATION.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BERRY JOE CORPORATION's MOTION For Court To Select And Appointment Of Arbitrator And Impose Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. David Finkelstein is appointed arbitrator with the powers to appoint an independent appraiser. Request for sanctions is denied. Moving party prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2017 Continued To Nov-08-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action
Off Calendar Mar-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Raymond Choy (415-387-5148) Defendant/opposing party, In Pro Per.
No other appearances.

The Court announces its tentative ruling. Defendant appeared for arbitration. Court explains the 1st issue can be artitrated re: Rent if lease is extended. 2nd issue is not subject to arbitration. Defendant states lease is not up until November of this year. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts and modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BERRY JOE CORPORATION's Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Motion is granted as to the first issue as it is subject to arbitration clause per paragraph 5(4). This appears store, Foods Etc & Berry Joe Corporation whose lease does not expire until November, is in Clear Lake - non-applicable. Clause in lease which is being enforced by Court is "If parties are unable to agree upon such an independent appraiser, either party may request AAA (American Arbitration Association) in San Francisco to appoint such an independent appraiser." Court instructs Defendant to submit proposed order today. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
